rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAIME E. ESTRADA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Money Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,060, And Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Compel Further Production is denied. First, there are no petitions or other litigation pending which would serve as a predicate for any discovery conducted by Petitioner with respect to the Mary Gianinni Trust. Secondly, the 1993 Mary Gianinni Trust and its amendments are private financial records protected from discovery. Finally, assuming that Respondent is entitled to know the specific terms defining Jamie Estrada's interest in the Mary Gianinni Trust (In re Marriage of Williamson), that information has already been provided to Petitioner by Respondent, i.e. the second and third amendments to the Trust, which specifically mention Mr. Estrada, as well as the Declaration under penalty of perjury of Betty Landi, executor of Mr. Estrada's Estate and Trustee of the Gianinni Trust, that no mention was made of Jaime Estrada, the son in law of Mary Gianinni, in either the original version of the Trust or the First Amendment. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, May 9, 2018.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERNEST V RHINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Standing Ruling In Order Sustaining Respondents Demurrer To Will Contest And Grounds Of Objection To Probate Of Will Of Ernest V. Rhine For Undue Influence; Request For Declaratory Relief As To Petitioner Being An Omitted Spouse
rulings.Rulings: The Motion for Reconsideration of Standing Ruling in Order Demurrer to Will Contest and Grounds of Objection to Probate of Will of Ernest V. Rhine for Undue Influence; Request for Declaratory Relief as to Petitioner Being an Omitted Spouse is denied. Respondents then failed to provide any new or different facts, circumstances, or law per CCP § 1008 and instead dispute the Court's interpretation of their previously cited authorities. Respondents cited to both In re Sankeys Estate (1926) 199 Cal. 391, and Katleman v. Crowley (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 51 in their demurrer. They make no showing that the Court did not consider the cited case law. They also make no showing that a case cited in the moving papers and at the hearing is a "new" law or fact for the purposes of a motion for reconsideration under CCP § 1008 because the Court did not choose to rely on it in making its ruling. Petitioner's request for sanctions under CCP §§ 1008 and 128.7 is denied as procedurally defective, as it is not brought as a separate motion and there is no showing that the required 21 day safe harbor period was observed. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, May 9, 2018.
